data.title: Mutual of America Financial Group partners with Morningstar Retirement, IRALOGIX, and Envestnet to launch customized IRA for investors.
data.description: In a collaborative effort, Mutual of America Financial Group, a prominent retirement services provider, has joined forces with Morningstar Retirement, IRALOGIX, and Envestnet to launch a personalized IRA option for investors. Set to debut in New York on September 18, 2025, this new offering aims to cater to individual and organizational needs. This partnership leverages the expertise of Morningstar Retirement, IRALOGIX, and Envestnet along with Mutual of America Financial Group’s established position in the industry. Investors can anticipate a tailored IRA experience with this alliance, marking a significant advancement in the realm of retirement planning.

data.title: American Battery Technology Co. triples quarterly revenue; achieves over 180% increase; focuses on critical mineral manufacturing.
data.description: A company experiences a substantial growth, with quarterly revenue soaring by over 180%. This growth is attributed to the company's efforts in ramping up commercial activities to enhance the domestic production of essential minerals.

data.title: Cracker Barrel's Q4 earnings fall short. Revenue: $868.09M, below expectations. Adjusted EPS: 74 cents, missing estimates.
data.description: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (NASDAQ: CBRL) released their disappointing fourth-quarter financial results with fourth-quarter revenue hitting $868.09 million, surpassing analyst projections of $855.30 million as reported by Benzinga Pro. However, the company fell short of expectations on adjusted earnings, reporting 74 cents per share compared to the anticipated 80 cents per share. Furthermore, their FY2026 sales forecast was below what analysts had predicted. Julie Masino, a spokesperson for the company, expressed gratitude towards customers for their feedback and loyalty, leading to decisions such as reverting to the ‘Old Timer’ logo, halting remodeling activities, and focusing on enhancing the overall guest experience. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Horizon Organic Dairy awards HOPE Award and scholarships, sponsors Farm Aid 40. Largest organic dairy brand in world.
data.description: Horizon Organic, a leading organic dairy brand based in Broomfield, Colorado, reaffirms its dedication to collaborating with farmers and advancing the organic dairy industry. As a strong supporter of Farm Aid 40, the company demonstrates its commitment to fostering partnerships with farmers. Horizon Organic, recognized as the largest USDA-certified organic milk and dairy brand globally, continues its mission to promote sustainable and organic farming practices. The company's proactive involvement in Farm Aid 40 signifies its ongoing efforts to support the agricultural community and sustainable food production.

data.title: Codoxo is a finalist in two categories at the 2025 A.I. Awards for their healthcare payment integrity model.
data.description: A healthcare payment integrity model developed by Point Zero has been acknowledged for its innovation and impact in the industry. Codoxo, a prominent provider of AI and generative AI-driven healthcare payment integrity solutions, revealed that it has been selected as a finalist for two awards. The recognition comes as a testament to the significant contribution made by the model, highlighting its effectiveness and advanced technology. This announcement was made in Duluth, Georgia on September 18, 2025.

data.title: illumynt, a CNE Direct company, promotes Jörg Herbarth to CEO. He aims to lead the company to become the top AI- focused ITAD provider globally.
data.description: In Burlington, Massachusetts, illumynt, a CNE Direct subsidiary, has promoted Jörg Herbarth to Chief Executive Officer (CEO). Jörg, who has been with illumynt since June 2022, previously held the position of Chief Operating Officer and is now leading the company towards becoming the leading provider of AI- and data-centric IT Asset Disposition (ITAD) solutions globally. This promotion signifies Jörg's growing role within the company's executive team.

data.title: AAR Corp. (NYSE: AIR) earnings expected to be $1.00/share, revenue $692.45 million. Shares fell 1.3% to $74.76. AAR subsidiary signs deal with Cebu Pacific.
data.description: AAR Corp (AIR) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings after the market closes on Tuesday, Sept. 23. Analysts predict earnings per share to reach $1.00, up from 85 cents a year earlier. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $692.45 million, compared to $661.7 million in the previous year. The company's subsidiary, Airinmar, has recently extended support services with Cebu Pacific, a low-cost carrier in the Philippines. Closing at $74.76, AAR's shares dropped by 1.3% on Wednesday. For those interested, the latest analyst ratings are available on the Analyst Stock Ratings page of Benzinga.

data.title: Fed cuts rates by 25 basis points, Goldman Sachs predicts more cuts in October & December, aiming for 3.00%–3.25% in 2026.
data.description: Goldman Sachs predicts that the recent 25-basis-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve is just the beginning of a series of reductions, with expectations of additional cuts in October and December. Chief U.S. economist David Mericle suggested that if the labor market weakens, a 50-basis-point cut is also possible. These cuts could potentially bring interest rates down to 3.00%–3.25% by the end of 2026. The move towards easing is in response to shifting economic risks from inflation to employment. The Federal Reserve's updated Summary of Economic Projections indicates a median forecast for three rate cuts this year, hinting at a more aggressive easing stance than initially anticipated by Goldman Sachs.

data.title: WRISE and Foley & Lardner LLP host AmyK Hutchens on Sep 24th for a presentation "Communicate to Connect" in San Diego, CA. Event free for renewables professionals.
data.description: A motivating networking opportunity is scheduled for Wednesday, September 24th, starting at 6:00pm and concluding at 7:30pm. The event will be held at Foley & Lardner, located at 11988 El Camino Real Suite 400 in San Diego, CA 92130. All professionals working in the renewables industry are invited to attend. Admission tickets and parking are provided at no cost. Limited space is available, so early registration is recommended.
